name: j-stack-skill-system
inspiration: Garry Tan / GStack-style operational skills; no affiliation or endorsement implied.
gbrain: Designed to use GBrain as newsroom memory, source graph, claim ledger, timeline, and publication record.

skills.name: jstack-command-center
skills.description: Use when starting, coordinating, or resuming a J-Stack journalism workflow. Routes the topic through editorial mission, evidence, drafting, red-team, legal/ethics, distribution, and post-publication follow-up.

skills.name: jstack-office-hours
skills.description: Use when testing whether a journalism idea, public thesis, investigation lead, or commentary concept is worth pursuing before spending research or drafting effort.

skills.name: jstack-editorial-line
skills.description: Use when an author, newsroom, or project has a public voice or worldview that should guide framing without changing evidence grades or factual findings.

skills.name: jstack-assignment-desk
skills.description: Use when breaking a journalism project into newsroom assignments, beats, deadlines, research tasks, review stages, and deliverables for humans or agents.

skills.name: jstack-source-map
skills.description: Use when building a complete source universe for a story: primary sources, secondary sources, adversarial sources, expert sources, missing sources, and archive status.

skills.name: jstack-primary-source-research
skills.description: Use when locating and interpreting primary public sources such as statutes, court records, official registers, corporate filings, sanctions lists, press releases, datasets, or original documents.

skills.name: jstack-osint-research
skills.description: Use when conducting lawful open-source research across public websites, social profiles, domains, corporate pages, media archives, map traces, public videos, and metadata visible to ordinary users.

skills.name: jstack-osint-source-router
skills.description: Use when selecting lawful public OSINT databases and public-record sources for a J-Stack story before live research. Routes questions to source classes, access/licensing/automation warnings, evidence limits, personal-data risk, citation fields, and claim-ledger next steps. Includes a seed OSINT Source Atlas and non-sensitive worked example.

skills.name: jstack-data-journalism
skills.description: Use when a story relies on datasets, tables, statistics, scraping outputs, budgets, filings, measurements, or repeatable quantitative analysis. Includes a non-sensitive worked example with a fictional dataset, reproducible calculation, limitations, and safe claim wording.

skills.name: jstack-interview-prep
skills.description: Use when preparing interviews, requests for comment, expert calls, hostile-subject questions, source briefings, or right-of-reply outreach drafts.

skills.name: jstack-document-review
skills.description: Use when extracting facts, parties, dates, claims, contradictions, and evidence from documents, PDFs, filings, contracts, letters, reports, screenshots, transcripts, or archives.

skills.name: jstack-investigate
skills.description: Use when testing a factual hypothesis, suspicion, allegation, or investigative lead before drafting. Splits claims, identifies evidence paths, and reports confidence honestly.

skills.name: jstack-claim-ledger
skills.description: Use when converting every publishable assertion into a controlled claim with evidence grade, counterevidence, safe wording, legal risk, and publication color.

skills.name: jstack-evidence-ledger
skills.description: Use when building a litigation-grade evidence record for facts, documents, quotes, datasets, interviews, screenshots, and public records that support or contradict claims.

skills.name: jstack-entity-graph
skills.description: Use when mapping people, organizations, publications, domains, funders, locations, aliases, relationships, and confidence levels in a story or investigation.

skills.name: jstack-timeline
skills.description: Use when reconstructing dated events, statements, filings, publications, decisions, denials, corrections, and consequences so a story does not imply false sequence or causality.

skills.name: jstack-countercase
skills.description: Use when building the best opposing case against a story, thesis, allegation, or draft before publication, including innocent explanations and subject-side arguments.

skills.name: jstack-legal-risk
skills.description: Use for a pre-publication legal-risk screen of journalism or public commentary, including defamation/libel, German Verdachtsberichterstattung, US First Amendment/defamation/privacy/anti-SLAPP/fair-use/source-protection posture, copyright, data protection, and active litigation risks.

skills.name: jstack-ethics-check
skills.description: Use when reviewing fairness, public interest, harm, vulnerable people, source protection, conflicts, framing, privacy, and correction duties before publication.

skills.name: jstack-draft
skills.description: Use when drafting news, analysis, opinion, investigation, feature, briefing, LinkedIn, X thread, or longform text from a prepared mission brief and claim ledger.

skills.name: jstack-copy-desk
skills.description: Use when tightening, restructuring, headline-testing, style-checking, and copyediting a draft without changing the evidence level or editorial mission.

skills.name: jstack-seo-distribution
skills.description: Use when turning a publishable story into search-intent, metadata, landing-page, newsletter, and audience-development material without overstating claims. Includes a non-sensitive worked example with a fictional story brief, search-intent map, metadata, newsletter copy, review gates, and metadata length/safety script.

skills.name: jstack-social-package
skills.description: Use when creating X, LinkedIn, newsletter, short-video, podcast, or thread drafts from a publication-ready story while preserving evidence boundaries and human approval.

skills.name: jstack-redteam
skills.description: Use for hostile pre-publication review by personas such as subject lawyer, skeptical editor, hostile opponent, platform moderator, fact checker, and ordinary intelligent reader.

skills.name: jstack-ship
skills.description: Use as the final publication gate for J-Stack work. Verifies mission, sources, claims, countercase, legal/ethics, red-team fixes, headlines, social snippets, GBrain record, and human approval.

skills.name: jstack-postpub
skills.description: Use after publication to monitor corrections, rebuttals, legal letters, new evidence, source rot, reader questions, follow-up stories, and GBrain timeline updates.

skills.name: jstack-gbrain-memory
skills.description: Use when turning J-Stack story artifacts into durable GBrain knowledge: story hubs, source cards, claim pages, evidence pages, entity graph links, timelines, corrections, and reusable background.
